title: Massachusetts law about eviction
section_headers: Other Massachusetts laws relating to eviction
content:
Massachusetts law about eviction

Laws, regulations, cases, and web sources on eviction law (summary process). Legal help is available.

## Best bets

Eviction for landlords and Eviction for tenants, Mass. Trial Court. Eviction guides written from the point of view of the landlord or the tenant.

Eviction basics and Notices to Quit, Mass. Legal Help, 2025. Answers common questions about the eviction process and notices to quit.

Legal tactics, Massachusetts Law Reform Institute, 2025. Chapter 12 covers evictions and includes forms.

### Eviction sealing
On May 5, 2025 a new law goes into effect that allows tenants in Massachusetts to "seal" their eviction record. Find out about eligibility and how to file a petition to have your record sealed.

## Legal assistance

Eviction legal services and mediation, Executive Office of Housing and Livable Communities. Available to both tenants and landlords.

Massachusetts legal resource finder, Legal Services Corp. Information for legal aid offices, government agencies and court programs that may be able to help you for free or at low cost.

Volunteer Lawyers Project. Call or request legal help online. VLP also offers in-person Housing Court clinics.

Boston only: Access to Counsel Program, a partnership between the Mayor's Office of Housing, Boston Public Schools, Family Aid Boston, and Greater Boston Legal Services. The program aims to prevent evictions for eligible households with children enrolled in K-12 schools by providing full legal representation for the family at risk of eviction.

## Massachusetts laws

Note: There are no laws against evicting, for valid reasons, tenants with children, tenants with disabilities, or evicting tenants during the winter season. However, if the eviction was determined by a judge to not be your fault, or if you can show a judge that you can't find a good place to live, you may be able to request a "Stay of Execution" for up to 6 months (or a year for tenants who are elderly or have a disability).

### Primary eviction law
MGL c. 239 Summary process for possession of land (eviction)

### New sections
MGL c. 239, s.16: People with eviction records on file in a court may petition to have such records sealed (Effective May 5, 2025)
MGL c. 239, s.17: Protections for impacted federal workers facing non-essential residential evictions and other penalties for nonpayment of rent during federal government shutdown (Effective November 25, 2025)

### Other Massachusetts laws relating to eviction
MGL c. 121B, s.32 Evictions, local housing authorities
MGL c. 139, s.19 Use of premises for illegal activities, voiding of lease
MGL c. 184, s.18 Forcible entry forbidden
MGL c. 186, s.11 Determination of lease for nonpayment of rent
MGL c. 186, s.11A Termination of lease for nonpayment of rent
MGL c. 186, s.12 Notice to determine estate at will
MGL c. 186, s.13 Recovery of possession after termination of tenancy at will
MGL c. 186, s.13A Tenants deemed to be at will upon foreclosure
MGL c. 186, s.14 Quiet enjoyment violation
MGL c. 186A Tenants in foreclosed properties

## Massachusetts regulations
105 CMR 410 State sanitary code - minimum standards of fitness for human habitation
940 CMR 3.00 General regulation
940 CMR 3.17(5) Unfair and deceptive acts by landlord

## Eviction process

To remove a tenant without the tenant's agreement a landlord must get permission from the court. A landlord cannot evict a tenant by physically removing the tenant or their personal property, or by changing the locks. The landlord must provide notice to the tenant, called a Notice to Quit.

The formal eviction process is called Summary Process. It begins when the landlord serves the tenant with a court document called a Summons and Complaint.

### Common defenses to eviction
- Failure to properly terminate the tenancy
- The landlord failed to correct known problems with the unit
- The landlord is evicting you in retaliation for activities protected by law
- Discrimination, including failure to make a reasonable accommodation for disability

### Emergency assistance
Apply for EA emergency family shelter if you are pregnant or have children under 21 years old.

Residential Assistance for Families in Transition (RAFT) provides short-term emergency rental assistance and funding to help with foreclosure, loss of utilities, and other housing emergencies.

### Tenants at sufferance
A tenant at sufferance is a tenant who does not have their landlord's permission to stay in their dwelling after the lease or agreement ends or after the landlord terminates tenancy with a notice to quit.

### Personal property after eviction
If the tenant does not move out and a law enforcement officer removes the tenant, the officer will also remove the tenant's belongings and place them in storage at the landlord's expense (landlord can seek reimbursement). The storage facility must hold the property for six months before disposing of it.
